title: OOF Sigmoid Logit Stacking of Orthogonal Experts
summary: The local-CV/public gap suggests model diversity is likely worth more than another round of feature inflation, but ROC AUC rewards preserved ranking.
rationale: The local-CV/public gap suggests model diversity is likely worth more than another round of feature inflation, but ROC AUC rewards preserved ranking. scikit-learn notes isotonic calibration can change AUC by creating ties, whereas sigmoid is strictly monotonic and preserves ranking.
implementation_hint: Generate out-of-fold predictions from three genuinely different models: the current best numeric booster, the race-lap ranker, and a native-categorical CatBoost expert using raw categorical combos rather than manual one-hot/frequency expansion. Fit sigmoid or temperature-style calibrators on OOF predictions only, then blend in logit space; avoid isotonic on the final blend.
expected_effect: More stable public-board performance via orthogonal model families and AUC-safe calibration.
risk: If base learners are too correlated, stacking adds noise; any calibrator trained on in-fold predictions will overstate CV.
